task_completion.description: A hexagon is sketched in the 2D view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A six-sided polygon is visible in the SolveSpace drawing area.

task_completion.description: The across-flats dimension of the hexagon is set to 10.0 mm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dimension constraint of '10.0' or '10' is visible between two opposite parallel lines of the hexagon.
The property browser (usually top left) indicates 'ok' for the degrees of freedom, meaning the sketch is fully constrained.

task_completion.description: The hexagon is extruded to a height of 15.0 mm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D hexagonal prism is visible, and the property browser shows an extrusion distance or height of '15.0' or '15'.

task_completion.description: The project is saved as hex_standoff.slvs in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or file manager shows 'hex_standoff.slvs' in '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/', or the SolveSpace window title shows this filename.

task_completion.description: The 3D model is exported as hex_standoff.stl in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog or file manager shows 'hex_standoff.stl' in '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/'.

integrity.description: The agent used the SolveSpace GUI to create the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show mouse clicks, menus, and drawing actions within the SolveSpace application.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to bypass the GUI creation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal is not used to generate the .slvs or .stl files directly via scripts.

integrity.description: The model is built step-by-step from scratch.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the progression from an empty canvas to a 2D sketch, then to a 3D extrusion.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output files are '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/hex_standoff.slvs' and '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/hex_standoff.stl'. The mathematical volume of the correct 3D model is approximately 1299.04 mm³. The across-flats dimension of the hexagon should be exactly 10.0 mm, and the extrusion height should be exactly 15.0 mm. A fully constrained sketch in SolveSpace typically shows 'ok' in the property browser instead of a number of degrees of freedom.
